PRO TEM JUDGE: ERNEST HALPERIN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PLAINTIFF ERNEST THAYER'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS IS UNWARRANTED, AND DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE NECESSARY FOR A CLASS CERTIFICATION MOTION. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT SEEKING ADDITIONAL LIMITED DISCOVERY TO CREATE AN ARGUMENT FOR CLASS TREATMENT OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS BASED UPON A STATISTICAL SAMPLE OF A PORTION OF THE CLASS. RATHER, PLAINTIFFS SEEK INSTEAD TO OBTAIN INDIVIDUALIZED EVIDENCE FROM EVERY SINGLE MEMBER OF PLAINTIFFS' PROPOSED CLASS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

Defendants Trinity Management Services And Trinity Properties'S Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-23-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew J. Wieger, Esq. and G. Ryan Patrick, Esq. of the Wiegel Law Group, Tel. 415.522.8230, for Defendant and moving party;
Joseph Wood, Esq. of Hennefer Finley & Wood, Tel. 415.421.6100, for Plaintiff opposing party.

Judge Miller disclosed that she knew Mr. Patrick when he worked in the court as an LRA for another judge but they did not have any social relationship. She believes this will not affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. There was no objection by either party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The court adopted the tentative ruling as modified. DEFENDANTS TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES and TRINITY PROPERTIES' Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery is granted. Order signed in open court. (See scanned order for details of the ruling.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Plaintiff Ernest Thayer'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-23-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph Wood, Esq. of Hennefer Finley & Wood, Tel. 415.421.6100, for Plaintiff and moving party;
Andrew J. Wiegel, Esq. and G. Ryan Patrick, Esq. of the Wiegel Law Group, Tel. 415.522.8230, for Defendant and opposing party.

Judge Miller disclosed that she knew Mr. Patrick when he worked in the court as an LRA for another judge but they did not have any social relationship. She believes this will not affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. There was no objection by either party.

RULING: The matter was argued. PLAINTIFF ERNEST THAYER'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is Denied. The discovery sought by Plaintiffs is unwarranted, and does not appear to be necessary for a class certification motion. Plaintiffs are not seeking additional limited discovery to create an argument for class treatment of Plaintiffs' claims based upon a statistical sample of a portion of the class. Rather, Plaintiffs seek instead to obtain individualized evidence from every single member of Plaintiffs' proposed class. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
